Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - The official compilation of the Qing Dynasty's Four Treasuries, a massive collection of Chinese literature.
  • 子部十二 - Refers to the eleventh category of the Four Treasuries, which includes various types of literature and texts.
  • 提要 - Means 'introduction' or 'preface,' often used to summarize or provide an overview of a text.
  • 集异记 - The title of the book, which translates to 'Collection of Strange Records,' indicating a compilation of unusual or supernatural stories.
  • 长庆中 - Refers to the Longqing period of the Tang Dynasty, a specific historical era.
  • 光州刺史 - Refers to the position of governor (刺史) of Guangzhou, a historical administrative region.
  • 河东 - Refers to Hedong, an ancient region in Shanxi province, often used as a geographical identifier.
  • 长庆 - Refers to the Longqing period, a specific era during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 薛用弱 - The name of the author, Xue Yongruo, a Tang Dynasty writer.
  • 仲胜 - The courtesy name of Xue Yongruo.
  • 徐佐卿化事 - Refers to a specific story involving Xu Zuoqing and a transformation (化), a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 狄仁杰集翠裘 - Refers to a story about Di Renjie and a green fur coat (集翠裘), another example of a supernatural or unusual tale.
  • 王维郁轮袍 - Refers to a story involving Wang Wei and a black robe (郁轮袍), another example of a supernatural or unusual tale.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定四库全书 - The 'Qinding Siku Quanshu' is a monumental collection of Chinese literature compiled during the Qing Dynasty, reflecting the cultural and intellectual achievements of China.
  • 子部十二 - The Four Treasuries are divided into four major categories, with 'xiaoshuo' (novels) being one of them. This classification helps organize and categorize different types of literature.
  • 集异记 - The title suggests that the book contains a collection of strange and supernatural stories, which were popular in Chinese literature for their entertainment and moral lessons.
  • 长庆中 - The Longqing period (821-824 AD) was a time of significant cultural and literary activity in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 光州刺史 - The position of governor was a high-ranking administrative role in the Tang Dynasty, responsible for governing a region.
  • 河东 - Hedong was an important region in ancient China, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 薛用弱 - Xue Yongruo was a Tang Dynasty writer known for his contributions to Chinese literature, particularly in the genre of supernatural tales.
  • 徐佐卿化事 - Xu Zuoqing was a historical figure, and the story of his transformation is a classic example of the supernatural tales found in Chinese literature.
  • 狄仁杰集翠裘 - Di Renjie was a famous Tang Dynasty official known for his wisdom and integrity. The story of his green fur coat is a popular tale in Chinese folklore.
  • 王维郁轮袍 - Wang Wei was a renowned Tang Dynasty poet and painter. The story of his black robe is another example of the supernatural tales that were popular during this period.

Proper Nouns

  • 钦定四库全书 - The official compilation of the Qing Dynasty's Four Treasuries.
  • 子部十二 - The eleventh category of the Four Treasuries.
  • 集异记 - The title of the book.
  • 长庆 - The Longqing period of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 光州刺史 - The position of governor of Guangzhou.
  • 河东 - The ancient region of Hedong.
  • 薛用弱 - The author of the book.
  • 仲胜 - The courtesy name of Xue Yongruo.
  • 徐佐卿 - A historical figure mentioned in the book.
  • 化事 - A story of transformation.
  • 狄仁杰 - A famous Tang Dynasty official.
  • 集翠裘 - A story involving a green fur coat.
  • 王维 - A renowned Tang Dynasty poet and painter.
  • 郁轮袍 - A story involving a black robe.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 積薪婦姑圍棊 - A story from classical literature where a woman plays chess with her husband while they are burning firewood. Illustrates a situation where two people are engaged in an activity while dealing with a practical task.
  • 王之涣旗亭画壁 - A story about the poet Wang Zhihuan, who inscribed poems on the walls of a tavern. Symbolizes the act of leaving one's mark or achieving recognition through one's work.
  • 諸事皆出此書 - Indicates that the book contains various stories and events, suggesting its importance as a source of historical and literary material.
  • 其良常山新宫铭 - Refers to a specific text or inscription associated with the 'New Palace of Changshan,' likely a historical record or literary work.
  • 洪迈随笔推为奇作 - Indicates that the scholar Hong Mai highly praises this work as a masterpiece, suggesting its literary value.
  • 小説家之可采者 - Suggests that the book is valuable for its literary content, particularly in the field of fiction.
  • 陈振孙书录解题谓是书一名古异记 - Indicates that the book is also known as 'Gu Yi Ji,' suggesting that it has multiple titles.
  • 然诸家着録俱无此名 - Indicates that other bibliographies do not mention this name, suggesting that the title 'Gu Yi Ji' is not widely recognized.
  • 不知振孙何本 - Indicates uncertainty about the source that Chen Zhensun used, suggesting that the title may not be widely known.
  • 唐比部郎中陆勲亦有集异记二卷 - Indicates that there was another work with a similar title, 'Ji Yi Ji,' written by Lu Xun during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 故文献通考题勲书为陆氏集异记以别于用弱书焉 - Explains how the two works were differentiated, with Lu Xun's book being titled 'Lushiji Yi Ji' to distinguish it from the book by Yong Ruo.
  • 博异记 - The title of the book being discussed, which means 'Records of Strange Events.'
  • 异闻之属臣 - Indicates that the book is a type of strange tales, suggesting its genre.
  • 不着姓氏 - Indicates that the author is unknown or not specified, suggesting that the book may be anonymous.
  • 考晁公武读书志 - Refers to the 'Dushu Zhi' by Chao Gongwu, a bibliographical work.
  • 老子指归十三卷 - Refers to a commentary on the 'Laozi' in thirteen volumes.
  • 与公武所言书名卷数皆合 - Means that the title and number of volumes match what Chao Gongwu mentioned, indicating consistency between sources.
  • 则谷神子其冯廓欤 - Means 'Then, could 'Gushenzi' be Feng Kuò?' A speculative statement about the identity of the author.
  • 胡应麟二酉缀遗 - Refers to the 'Er You Zhuiyi' by Hu Yinglin, a work that discusses various literary topics.
  • 唐诗人郑还古尝为殷七七作传 - Means 'During the Tang Dynasty, the poet Zheng Huangu wrote a biography of Yin Qiqi.' Provides context for the authorship of the work.
  • 其人正晚唐 - Means 'He was a late Tang Dynasty figure,' indicating the time period of the author.
  • 盖其作也 - Means 'This is likely his work,' indicating that the author is the same person.
  • 其説亦似有依据 - Means 'This explanation seems to have some basis,' suggesting that the claim about the authorship is plausible.
  • 然古无明文 - Means 'However, there is no clear evidence from ancient sources,' indicating uncertainty about the claim.
  • 阙所不知可矣 - Means 'It is acceptable to leave unknown things unknown,' suggesting that the translator should not speculate beyond the available evidence.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 之刘方元马燧十人 - Lists ten individuals, including Liu Fangyuan and Ma Su, but the exact context is unclear.
  • 太平广记三百四十八巻 - Refers to the *Tài Píng Guǎng Jì* (Comprehensive Records of the Great Peace), a vast collection of stories and anecdotes from the Song Dynasty, specifically volume 348.
  • 载李全质一条 - Means 'contains a section about Li Quanzhi,' indicating a specific entry in the *Tài Píng Guǎng Jì*.
  • 称防昌壬戍济隂大水 - Refers to a flood in Jiyin (济阴) during the year of Ren Xu (壬戍) in the reign of Emperor Fanchang (防昌), a period of natural disaster.
  • 谷神子与全质同舟云云 - Means 'the god of the grain and Li Quanzhi were in the same boat,' suggesting a supernatural event.
  • 此本无之 - Means 'this version does not include it,' indicating a difference between versions of the text.
  • 盖亦钞合而成非完帙也 - Means 'it seems to be a compilation and not a complete work,' suggesting the text is a collection of fragments.
  • 所记皆神怪之事 - Means 'what it records are all supernatural events,' indicating the nature of the content.
  • 叙述雅赡 - Means 'the narrative is elegant and well-structured,' praising the style.
  • 所録诗歌颇工致 - Means 'the recorded poems are quite skillful,' indicating the quality of the poetry.
  • 视他小説为胜 - Means 'it is superior to other novels,' comparing the quality of the work.
  • 惟师旷镜铭一条不似三代语尔 - Means 'only the inscription on the mirror by Shi Kuang does not resemble the language of the Three Dynasties,' indicating a stylistic inconsistency.
  • 陈振孙书録解题谓语触时忌故其名 - Means 'Chen Zhen Sun, in his *Book Catalogue and Commentary*, said that the work touched on contemporary taboos, hence its name was omitted,' suggesting censorship.
  • 前有自序亦称非徒但资笑语抑亦麤显箴规 - Means 'there is a preface that also says it is not just for entertainment but also provides rough and clear advice,' indicating the dual purpose of the work.
  • 或冀逆耳之词稍获周身之戒 - Means 'or perhaps to gain some protection by hearing unpleasant words,' suggesting the author's intent to provide warnings.
  • 今观所载殊不见触忌之语 - Means 'now, looking at what is recorded, it is not obvious that it touches on taboos,' indicating a discrepancy between the claim and the content.
  • 而证以太平广记所引又确为本书非出依托 - Means 'but when verified by the citations in the *Tài Píng Guǎng Jì*, it is indeed this book and not a fabrication,' confirming the authenticity of the text.
  • 未审其寓言之防何在也 - Means 'it is unclear where the allegory lies,' indicating a question about the deeper meaning of the text.
  • 乾隆四十六年十二月恭校上 - Means 'in the twelfth month of the forty-sixth year of the Qianlong era, respectfully submitted for review,' indicating the date and context of the submission.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 刘方元 - A historical figure.
  • 马燧 - A historical figure.
  • 李全质 - A historical figure whose story is mentioned in the *Tài Píng Guǎng Jì*.
  • 防昌 - A historical figure, Emperor Fanchang.
  • 壬戍 - A Chinese calendar term.
  • 济隂 - A historical place.
  • 谷神子 - A supernatural figure, likely a deity or spirit, associated with grain.
  • 师旷 - A historical figure, a blind musician and advisor to several Chinese kings, known for his wisdom and musical talent.
  • 陈振孙 - A historical figure known for his work *Book Catalogue and Commentary*.
  • 纪昀 - A historical figure.
  • 陆锡熊 - A historical figure.
  • 孙士毅 - A historical figure.
  • 陆费墀 - A historical figure.
  • 乾隆四十六年 - A historical year.
  • 太平广记 - A historical text.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘方元 - A historical figure.
  • 马燧 - A historical figure.
  • 李全质 - A historical figure.
  • 防昌 - A historical figure, Emperor Fanchang.
  • 壬戍 - A Chinese calendar term.
  • 济隂 - A historical place.
  • 谷神子 - A supernatural figure.
  • 师旷 - A historical figure.
  • 陈振孙 - A historical figure.
  • 纪昀 - A historical figure.
  • 陆锡熊 - A historical figure.
  • 孙士毅 - A historical figure.
  • 陆费墀 - A historical figure.
  • 乾隆四十六年 - A historical year.
  • 太平广记 - A historical text.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 益州城距郭十五里有明月观焉依山临水松桂深寂道流非修习精悫者莫得而居观之东廊第一院尤为幽绝 - Fifteen miles from the city of Yizhou, there is a Moonlight Temple by the mountains and waters, surrounded by deep and quiet pines and cedars. Only those who have diligently practiced can live here. The first courtyard on the east corridor is especially secluded and beautiful.
  • 一日忽自外至神爽不怡谓院中人曰吾行山中偶为飞矢所加寻已无恙矣然此箭非人间所有吾留之于壁上后年箭主到此即宜付之慎无坠失仍援毫记壁云留箭之日则十三载九月九日也 - One day, he suddenly arrived from outside, looking troubled. He told the people in the courtyard, 'While traveling in the mountains, I was accidentally hit by an arrow, but I am now fine. However, this arrow is not from the human world. I left it on the wall. When the owner of the arrow comes back in two years, you should give it to him. Be careful not to lose it. I also wrote on the wall, 'The day the arrow was left is the ninth day of the ninth month of the thirteenth year.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐 - Refers to the Tang Dynasty (618–907 CE).
  • 薛用弱 - A Tang Dynasty writer and compiler.
  • 明皇 - Emperor Xuanzong (Li Longji), the fourth emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 天宝十三载 - The thirteenth year of the Tianbao era (754 CE).
  • 重阳日 - The Double Ninth Festival, celebrated on the ninth day of the ninth lunar month.
  • 沙苑 - A place in Shaanxi Province, known for its hunting grounds.
  • 益州 - An ancient name for Chengdu, the capital of Sichuan Province.
  • 明月观 - A temple in Yizhou, known for its serene and beautiful environment.
  • 避狄 - Fleeing from the nomadic tribes, specifically the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 幸蜀 - Visiting Sichuan.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐 - Tang Dynasty.
  • 薛用弱 - Xue Yongruo.
  • 明皇 - Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 天宝十三载 - Thirteenth year of the Tianbao era.
  • 重阳日 - Double Ninth Festival.
  • 沙苑 - Shayan.
  • 益州 - Yizhou (Chengdu).
  • 明月观 - Moonlight Temple.
  • 避狄 - Fleeing from the nomads.
  • 幸蜀 - Visiting Sichuan.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 王积薪 - Historical figure known for his skill in wei qi (Go).
  • 南狩 - Euphemism for fleeing or escaping, often used in the context of emperors or rulers.
  • 百司奔赴行在 - Indicates that officials rushed to the emperor's temporary residence.
  • 翰林善围棊者 - Refers to skilled players of wei qi (Go) in the imperial academy.
  • 蜀道隘狭 - Describes the narrow and difficult roads in the Shu region.
  • 栖栖而无所入 - Literary expression meaning to be restless and without a place to settle.
  • 寓宿于山中孤姥之家 - Indicates staying overnight in the home of a solitary old woman in the mountains.
  • 但有妇姑止给水火 - Means that only the wife and daughter-in-law were present, providing water and firewood.
  • 每置一子皆良久思惟 - Describes the careful and thoughtful placement of each piece in the game of wei qi.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王积薪 - Historical figure known for his skill in wei qi, a strategic board game that was highly valued in ancient China.
  • 南狩 - Term often used in historical contexts to describe the emperor's escape or relocation, especially during times of crisis.
  • 百司 - Refers to the various officials and government departments.
  • 行在 - Temporary residence of the emperor, often used when the emperor is on the move.
  • 翰林 - Imperial academy, a prestigious institution where scholars and literati served.
  • 蜀道 - Roads in the Shu region, known for their difficulty and narrowness.
  • 孤姥 - Solitary old woman, often used in literature to evoke a sense of isolation or hardship.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Wang Jixi followed him, where 'him' refers to the emperor.
  • The sentence describes the difficult conditions on the road, with the phrase '多为尊官有力者之所见占' indicating that the accommodations are often occupied by powerful officials.
  • This sentence describes Wang Jixi's situation, with the phrase '栖栖而无所入' indicating his restlessness and lack of a place to stay.
  • This phrase is elliptical, meaning 'only the wife and daughter-in-law were present, providing water and firewood.'
  • This phrase describes the careful and thoughtful placement of each piece in the game of wei qi.

Proper Nouns

  • 王积薪 - Name of a historical figure known for his skill in wei qi.
  • 南狩 - Term used to describe the emperor's escape or relocation.
  • 百司 - Refers to the various officials and government departments.
  • 行在 - Temporary residence of the emperor.
  • 翰林 - Imperial academy.
  • 蜀道 - Roads in the Shu region.
  • 孤姥 - Solitary old woman.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 将尽四更 - Refers to the late hours of the night, specifically near the fourth watch of the night.
  • 积薪 - A term that can be interpreted as 'a pile of firewood' but in this context likely refers to a person or entity named 'Jixi.'
  • 忽闻姑曰 - Means 'suddenly heard the aunt say.'
  • 子已败矣 - Means 'you have already lost' or 'you have already failed.'
  • 吾止胜九枰耳 - Means 'I only won nine boards' or 'I only managed to win nine moves.'
  • 妇亦甘焉 - Means 'the wife also accepted it' or 'the wife also agreed.'
  • 迟明具衣冠 - Means 'at dawn, dressed in formal attire.'
  • 请问孤姥 - Means 'asked the old lady.'
  • 尔可率已之意而按局置子焉 - Means 'you may follow your own intention and place the pieces according to the board.'
  • 具衣冠 - Means 'dressed in formal attire.'
  • 罄竭心力 - Means 'exhausted all efforts' or 'used all one's strength.'
  • 较其九枰之胜 - Means 'compared the victory of the nine boards' or 'compared the success of the nine moves.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邓艾开蜀势 - Refers to the historical event where Deng Ai, a general of the Wei state, defeated the Shu state during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 其图有焉 - Refers to a diagram or plan that exists, possibly a strategic map or a game board.

Proper Nouns

  • 积薪 - Likely a person or entity named 'Jixi.'
  • 姑 - An aunt or elder female relative.
  • 妇 - A wife or female relative.
  • 孤姥 - An old lady or elder female figure.
  • 邓艾 - A historical figure, a general of the Wei state during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 开蜀势 - A strategic opening of the Shu state.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 平等閣 - The name of the structure, which is a temple or pagoda.
  • 釋子 - Refers to a Buddhist monk.
  • 澄空 - The name of the monk, indicating a person of spiritual significance.
  • 年甫二十 - Means 'just turned twenty.'
  • 誓願 - Refers to a vow or aspiration.
  • 於晉陽汾西 - Indicates the location, 'in the west of Fen River near Jin Yang.'
  • 鑄鐵像 - Means 'to cast an iron statue.'
  • 高七十尺 - Specifies the height of the statue, 'seventy feet tall.'
  • 鸠集金炭 - Means 'to gather iron and coal.'
  • 细求用度 - Means 'to meticulously calculate expenses.'
  • 周二十年 - Indicates the duration, 'over twenty years.'
  • 物力乃办 - Means 'the resources were finally prepared.'
  • 告报遐迩 - Means 'to announce far and wide.'
  • 大集贤愚 - Means 'to gather the wise and the foolish.'
  • 选日而写像 - Means 'to choose a day to cast the statue.'
  • 烟焰息灭 - Means 'the smoke and flames subsided.'
  • 啓鑪之后 - Means 'after opening the furnace.'
  • 其像无成 - Means 'the statue was not completed.'
  • 深自咎责 - Means 'to deeply blame oneself.'
  • 稽首懴悔 - Means 'to prostrate oneself and repent.'
  • 复坚前约 - Means 'to reaffirm the previous vow.'
  • 再谋铸造 - Means 'to plan another casting.'
  • 精勤艰苦 - Means 'to work diligently and arduously.'
  • 事费复备 - Means 'the expenses were again prepared.'
  • 大加贬挫 - Means 'to severely criticize oneself.'
  • 深自勤励 - Means 'to strive hard and encourage oneself.'
  • 功力复集 - Means 'the effort was again gathered.'
  • 扬声谓观者 - Means 'to speak loudly to the spectators.'
  • 吾少发誓愿 - Means 'I made a vow when I was young.'
  • 今年八十 - Means 'this year, I am eighty.'
  • 两已不成 - Means 'two attempts have failed.'
  • 此更违心 - Means 'this goes against my heart.'
  • 吾无身以终志矣 - Means 'I will not have a body to fulfill my vow.'
  • 况今众善虚费积 - Means 'how much more so the current good deeds that are wasted.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 隋开皇中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Wen of Sui (581-604 CE).
  • 晋阳 - An ancient name for Taiyuan, a city in Shanxi Province.
  • 汾西 - Refers to the western bank of the Fen River.
  • 铸铁像 - Refers to the practice of casting iron statues, which was a significant religious and artistic activity in ancient China.
  • 告报遐迩 - Refers to the practice of announcing events to a wide audience, often through public proclamations or announcements.
  • 大集贤愚 - Refers to the gathering of people from all walks of life, emphasizing inclusivity.
  • 选日而写像 - Refers to the ritual of choosing a propitious day for a significant event, such as casting a statue.
  • 烟焰息灭 - Refers to the completion of the casting process, where the furnace is opened after the metal has cooled.

Proper Nouns

  • 隋开皇中 - The reign period of Emperor Wen of Sui.
